meta_title: For Founders: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage user onboarding to overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. An advanced tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS founders.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, build an interactive onboarding flow that captures user intent before you even have a product. By mapping the friction points of your target market into a guided discovery process, you turn a blank canvas into a validated demand-generation machine for your next SaaS.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. The 'idea' you have feels thin, uninspired, and frankly, risky. Most founders stall here, paralyzed by the fear of building for an audience that doesn't exist. The graveyard of SaaS is filled with products that solved problems nobody had.

But what if the product wasn't the starting point? What if you built a sophisticated onboarding experience first—a digital concierge that maps user pain points in real-time? By shifting your focus to the user's journey, you transform the 'no project idea' dilemma into a high-fidelity data collection engine, ensuring that when you do write the first line of core logic, you are building exactly what users have already begged you for.

steps.title: Define the Friction Map
steps.description: Identify the top three pain points in your target niche. We will build a diagnostic onboarding flow that forces users to rank these points, providing you with quantitative market validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-step diagnostic onboarding flow that captures user pain points and maps them to potential SaaS feature sets. Use a modular component structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate onboarding-schema.json using the provided pain-point mapping architecture'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Discovery Layer
steps.description: Implement a UI that feels less like a sign-up form and more like a high-end consultant. This increases conversion by framing the product as a solution to their specific input.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React-based onboarding progress bar and interactive questionnaire component with smooth transitions and state management for user inputs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'onboarding-wizard' component using Tailwind and Framer Motion

steps.title: Implement Intent-Based Logic
steps.description: Don't just collect data; act on it. Use the user's answers to dynamically adjust the UI, creating a personalized experience that builds immediate trust.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript hook that takes user onboarding responses and returns a 'personalized_dashboard_config' object to hide irrelevant features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'useOnboardingLogic' hook with conditional rendering logic based on user survey results

steps.title: Integrate Behavioral Analytics
steps.description: Track where users drop off in your onboarding. This reveals which parts of your 'idea' are actually valuable to the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a segment-friendly analytics wrapper that tracks step-by-step completion rates within the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'analytics-middleware' for tracking onboarding drop-off events

steps.title: Automated Feedback Loop
steps.description: Connect your onboarding data to a backend service that synthesizes user intent into a 'Feature Roadmap' document.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a node.js script that aggregates daily onboarding survey results and generates a JSON summary of top-requested features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'summarize-user-feedback' and append to roadmap.md

pro_tips: Never ask for an email until after the user has received value from your discovery flow.
Use 'micro-commitments' in onboarding; ask for low-effort binary choices before deep-dive qualitative input.
If your drop-off rate exceeds 40% on a single step, that step is likely asking for information the user doesn't feel comfortable giving yet.
Pre-fill onboarding inputs based on URL parameters to make the process feel hyper-personalized from the first click.
Treat your onboarding flow as your MVP—if users won't finish the flow, they won't use the product.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, run 50 users through it via cold outreach, and analyze the 'Feature Roadmap' report generated by your new system.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop guessing and start building with intent. By treating your onboarding as a discovery tool, you eliminate the risk of building unwanted software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
